headline: Robinhood Shares Jump After Company Announces $1.5 Billion Stock Buyback Plan
summary: Shares of  Robinhood  Markets jumped 7% Wednesday morning on news the brokerage firm’s board of directors has approved a new $1.5 billion stock buyback program.  The company said late Tuesday that it anticipates that the buyback program will be executed over the next three years, with flexibility to accelerate if market conditions warrant.  “This authorization reflects the confidence of our management team and board in our ability to continue delivering innovative products for customers and creating value for shareholders while returning capital over time,” Shiv Verma, chief financial officer of Robinhood, said in a statement.

headline: Prediction Market Rivals Kalshi and Polymarket Set Aside Bitter Feud to Back $35M VC Fund
summary: Kalshi and Polymarket are unusually backing a new venture, 5(c) Capital, despite their well-known ongoing rivalry in the industry. The fund is being launched by former Kalshi employees to raise $35 million for its first investment vehicle, which will support startups in the prediction market ecosystem. headline: These Stocks Are Today’s Movers: Arm, Intel, Meta, Alibaba, JD.com, Robinhood, GameStop, and More
summary: Stocks were rising Wednesday as investors held out hope for a swift end to the war in the Middle East amid cease-fire talks between the U.S. and Iran.  While shares initially fell Tuesday when the company unveiled its plans to enter the market for data-center chips, Arm laid out a series of aggressive sales targets for the coming years in after-hours trading, boosting shares.  Intel  and  Advanced Micro Devices  rose 6.9% and 6.6%, respectively.
